title: Bulenox Pricing Explained: All Plans, Costs & Fees (2026)

excerpt: Complete breakdown of Bulenox pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Bulenox Pricing Overview

Bulenox is a futures proprietary trading firm founded in 2022 that offers funded trading accounts ranging from $10,000 to $250,000. The platform charges monthly subscription fees to access these accounts, with pricing that varies based on account size.

The starting price is $115 per month for the smallest $10K account tier. This entry-level cost is competitive within the prop firm space, particularly given Bulenox's standout profit-sharing model: traders retain 100% of profits on the first $10,000 earned, with a 90/10 split (90% trader, 10% firm) on anything above that threshold.

Unlike some competitors, Bulenox does not offer a free tier or trial period. You must commit to a monthly subscription to access a funded account. However, the company frequently runs promotions offering discounts of up to 75% off monthly fees, which can significantly reduce your initial entry cost.

All Bulenox Plans Compared

Bulenox offers six distinct account tiers, each with different monthly fees based on the account size. Here's the complete breakdown:

Account Size Monthly Fee Estimated Annual Cost Best For
$10K $115/month $1,380 Beginner traders, tight budgets
$25K $143/month $1,716 Intermediate traders, scalp strategies
$50K $195/month $2,340 Day traders, swing traders
$100K $295/month $3,540 Experienced day traders, high-volume trading
$150K $395/month $4,740 Professional traders, algorithmic trading
$250K $535/month $6,420 Elite traders, large position sizing

All six tiers unlock access to Bulenox's complete feature set, including:

  • Custom indicators and charting tools
  • Automated trading and Expert Advisor (EA) support
  • Performance analytics and reporting
  • Risk management tools
  • Access to 42 futures instruments (including Micro Bitcoin access)
  • NinjaTrader 8 platform integration

The difference between tiers is purely the account balance size and the corresponding monthly subscription cost. A trader on the $10K tier has access to the same tools and markets as someone trading the $250K account.

Free Plan / Free Trial

Bulenox does not offer a free tier or free trial. You must pay the monthly subscription fee to access any funded account, regardless of account size.

However, this doesn't mean you have no way to evaluate the platform. Bulenox offers a critical advantage: no time limit on the evaluation phase. Unlike many prop firms that force traders to reach profitability within 30, 60, or 90 days, Bulenox allows you to trade the funded account as long as you need—there's no countdown clock pressuring you to hit profit targets quickly.

This is particularly valuable if you're:

  • Testing a new trading strategy
  • Learning to trade futures for the first time
  • Migrating from another market (stocks, forex, crypto spot)
  • Fine-tuning an algorithmic trading system

The extended evaluation timeline effectively reduces the cost of learning, since you're only paying monthly fees—not racing against arbitrary time limits.

Hidden Costs and Fees

Bulenox's pricing model is relatively transparent, but there are several costs to factor in beyond the monthly subscription:

  • Exchange Fees: Futures trades incur standard exchange fees. Bulenox does not explicitly waive these, though the exact amounts depend on the contract and exchange. Factor in approximately $0.50–$2.00 per micro contract round-trip trade, depending on the futures instrument.
  • Data Feeds: Real-time market data feeds for NinjaTrader 8 may carry additional costs beyond the platform subscription, depending on your broker and data provider settings within the Bulenox ecosystem.
  • Commissions: While not always itemized separately, futures commissions are deducted from your trading profits. Bulenox's profit-sharing structure (100% on first $10K, then 90/10) applies after commissions are taken.
  • Monthly Fees Until Mastery: The most significant hidden cost is this: monthly fees recur every month until you reach the "Master phase." If your evaluation takes 6 months, you'll pay the monthly fee six times. On a $50K account, that's $1,170 in fees just to get approved for live trading. This is a deliberate model choice by Bulenox—they make money from subscriptions, not just profit splits—but it's important to understand the cumulative cost.
  • No Withdrawal of Profits During Evaluation: Until you're approved to trade a live account, profits remain locked in the evaluation account. You cannot withdraw earnings mid-evaluation, so plan accordingly for cash flow.

Bulenox Pricing vs Competitors

How does Bulenox stack up against other futures prop firms? Here's a comparison with two major competitors:

Firm $25K Account Profit Split Free Trial?
Bulenox $143/month 100% on first $10K, then 90/10 No
Axiom Trading ~$199/month (est.) 80/20 or 85/15 No
Topstep Futures ~$119/month (est.) 50/50 Yes (14-day trial)

Verdict: Bulenox sits in the middle of the pricing spectrum. It's slightly cheaper than Axiom but slightly more expensive than Topstep. The real differentiator is the 100% profit retention on the first $10,000—this is an industry-leading profit split that no major competitor currently matches. If you can reach a $10K profit quickly, Bulenox's split structure becomes significantly more valuable than competitors offering 50/50 or 80/20 from day one.

For more detailed comparisons, see our Futures Prop Firm Comparison page.

Is Bulenox Worth the Price?

For Futures Day Traders: Yes. If you're actively trading futures and can reach $10K in profits, Bulenox's 100% retention structure is unbeatable. The $143–$295 monthly cost is quickly recovered when you're making 10–20 trades per day and keeping all profits up to $10K.

For Algorithmic Traders: Absolutely. Bulenox explicitly permits Expert Advisors and automated strategies, which is rare among prop firms. Combined with access to 42 futures instruments and no evaluation time limit, this is an excellent platform for testing and scaling algo systems. The monthly fee becomes negligible when you're running profitable bots 24/5.

For Crypto Futures Traders: Yes, especially if you need leveraged trading capital. Bulenox supports crypto futures, which most traditional prop firms don't. The $250K top tier with a $535/month fee is still cheaper than running equivalent capital through a crypto exchange's leverage system.

For Beginners or Casual Traders: Maybe not. If you're still learning to trade consistently profitable strategies, the monthly fees add up fast. A six-month evaluation phase on a $10K account costs $690—money you won't recover unless you reach profitability. Consider starting with paper trading or a smaller account elsewhere to prove your edge first.

For Swing Traders: The $50K account at $195/month is a solid entry point. Your lower trade frequency means lower commissions, and the extended evaluation period reduces pressure. If you make just 2–3 winning trades per month averaging +$500 profit each, you'll recoup the monthly fee immediately.

How to Save on Bulenox

Promotional Discounts: Bulenox frequently runs promotions offering up to 75% off monthly fees. If a promotion is active, a $115/month account can drop to as low as ~$29 per month. This transforms the entry cost: instead of paying $1,380 annually, you'd pay ~$348. Check the Bulenox website for current promo codes before subscribing.

Quarterly or Annual Prepayment: Some prop firms offer discounts for annual prepayment. Confirm with Bulenox whether paying 3, 6, or 12 months upfront unlocks a discount—this could save 10–20% on total fees.

Start Smaller, Upgrade Later: Begin on the $10K account ($115/month) while you're learning the platform. Once you're consistently profitable, upgrade to a larger tier. This reduces your cost during the evaluation phase and lets you scale when you're ready.

Combine with Performance Bonuses: Watch for seasonal or milestone-based bonuses. If Bulenox offers deposit bonuses or achievement-based credits, these can offset monthly subscription costs.

Summary: Bulenox's pricing ranges from $115 to $535 per month, making it an affordable option for serious futures traders. The standout 100% profit split on the first $10K earned justifies the monthly cost if you're profitable. With no time limit on evaluation and support for algorithmic trading, Bulenox is particularly valuable for traders seeking capital and willing to pay monthly fees in exchange for a superior profit-sharing structure.

Ready to explore Bulenox further? Visit our Bulenox review page or compare it to other prop firms in our database.
